title: 1.1 Reports how maddy handles a message body containing an embedded single dot line (e.g., content before dot, ".", content after dot)
1.2: States whether maddy stops reading at the first dot or continues consuming input (e.g., continues to final terminator, does not stop early).
1.3: Reports what actually gets stored after processing a message with embedded dots (e.g., byte count, stored body content).
1.4: Identifies runtime signs showing which parsing path was taken (e.g., log entries, msg_id, acceptance messages).
1.5: Explains how authentication state persists after RSET (e.g., AuthUser remains in connection state, not cleared by reset).
1.6: Reports whether maddy allows different sender identity after RSET without re-authentication (e.g., accepts MAIL FROM with user2 while authenticated as user1).
1.7: Identifies which identity is recorded in queue metadata (e.g., MsgMeta.OriginalFrom for envelope sender, MsgMeta.Conn.AuthUser for authenticated user).
1.8: Reports how both identities appear in log entries (e.g., sender field shows envelope address, username field shows authenticated user)
1.9: States whether the system fails safely when faced with protocol misuse (e.g., maintains audit trail, preserves both identities).
1.10: Explains how dot-stuffing protocol works (e.g., ".." on wire becomes "." in storage, client escapes dots).
1.11: Reports the RSET behavior regarding transaction state (e.g., clears MAIL FROM, recipients, delivery context).
